title: card.rs: extract_first_url returns Option<String> instead of Option<&str>
description:
LOCATION: src/card.rs lines 19-24

FINDING: extract_first_url(html: &str) -> Option<String> returns an owned String via .map(|m| m.as_str().to_string()). The match result m.as_str() is a &str slice of the input html. The function signature could return Option<&str> borrowing from html, eliminating the allocation.

All call sites either immediately use the URL as &str or pass it to an async closure that needs ownership — but the caller can call .to_owned() only when needed rather than always allocating.

This is the same class as the extract_first_url returns String finding from pass 1 (which the instructions say was already filed for a different function). Verify it was not already filed for THIS function in card.rs specifically.
